recommendations.rationale: The failure was reported pre-existing on the RFX-04 working tree. On the current commit (ff1b6bb1) it is non-reproducible across two consecutive runs and across the broader pattern run. Marking 'no_change_failure_not_reproduced' is permitted because the failure is consistently absent — not flaky.
recommendations.guard_against_dodge: Listed in BLF gate so any future re-emergence becomes a blocking signal.

recommendations.rationale: Test fixture _init_repo silently couples to host commit.gpgsign config. Fixture is at fault, not the production analyzer. Pinning local commit.gpgsign=false restores deterministic isolation without weakening any production guard or schema. Stricter than the previous fixture: it now denies the host the ability to influence subprocess git commit semantics.

Same fixture-isolation gap as test_contract_impact_analysis. Pinning local commit.gpgsign=false in _init_git_repo restores determinism. The production governed-autofix module is intentionally NOT modified to avoid widening BLF-01 scope into an authority-vocabulary cleanup of pre-existing tokens; because the production code commits inside the fixture-sealed temp repo, the fixture pin is sufficient.

The runner's PQX-equivalent execution authority requires deterministic reproduction of behavioral proofs. PATH-resolved pytest is non-deterministic. The fix pins behavioral subprocess pytest invocations to sys.executable. Strictly safer: it eliminates an implicit PATH dependency without changing the contract's classification or review-eligibility surface.
